body.title: Echo timing: sync palace → files + GitHub on ticket close, not at day's end
body.result: Extended cc_queue.py cmd_done with _append_to_todays_slate helper: appends closed ticket to today's ## Done today section (idempotent, graceful-degrade). Combined with pre-existing queue.json echo regen on _save, palace→disk sync now happens at ticket close instead of only at day-close. Scope tight: decisions_log.dsb echo + retroactive-ticketing prompt deferred (separate work).
body.description:
Today: palace_sync, docs_sync, github_sync run at /day-close. That means files are stale all day.

New: every ticket close triggers the sync. Files on disk, GitHub issues, decisions log, slate echo are always current within seconds of the state change.

## Also includes: retroactive ticket creation on close

Akien 2026-04-20: 'sort of like you saying oh, and i also fixed this'. The close action handles incidental fixes — if a sprint touched something that didn't have a ticket, the close step offers to file+immediately-close a ticket for it. Preserves 'every change has a ticket' without forcing pre-sprint ticketing for small debris fixes.

## Changes
- cc_queue.py done action → triggers palace→disk sync (tickets, decisions, slates, skills)
- /sprint step 9 (close) detects 'additional commits that weren't part of the claimed ticket' and prompts: 'file T-incidental-xxx?'
- Graceful degrade: if sync fails, log locally, retry on next close; don't block the close

## Blocked by
- T-palace-as-source-of-truth (storage shape has to exist)
- All the -into-palace-subtree tickets (the subtrees have to exist)
